title: GitHub remote MCP (http) with an Authorization Bearer header → UNKNOWN

origin.description: Public documentation config from github/github-mcp-server (README.md, remote http variant with an Authorization header). Verbatim except normalization to a valid JSON root; the bearer value is upstream's own VS Code input placeholder `${input:github_mcp_pat}`.

humanExpectation.rationale: A recognized remote endpoint (https://api.githubcopilot.com/mcp/) is uninspectable by construction — CallLint never connects to it — so the verdict is UNKNOWN, never SAFE (ADR 0010). The host is allowlisted, so supply.unknown-remote does NOT fire and there are zero findings; UNKNOWN comes purely from the sourceKnown gate. This is the Authorization-header variant of the GitHub remote: contrast C025 (same host, no header → UNKNOWN, 0 findings) and C026 (GitHub Enterprise host, non-allowlisted → UNKNOWN WITH supply.unknown-remote). It pins that an inline Authorization: Bearer header on an allowlisted remote does not by itself add a finding or downgrade the UNKNOWN.
knownLimitations: CallLint does not parse the Authorization header value as a secret (it is a header, not an env key, and the value is a placeholder); the header is recorded as part of the config shape only. The remote is uninspectable, so UNKNOWN is the honest verdict regardless of the header.
